summary: First iteration end-to-end testing of PlaySphere. Backend: 31/31 pytest tests passed (auth cookie+JWT, events/teams/players/sponsors CRUD, RBAC, round-robin (n*(n-1)/2) & knockout fixture generation with winner propagation, standings calculation, /api/stats, /api/ metadata, seed data verification). Frontend: home hero tagline, stat strip, event listing (3 demo events), event detail tabs (fixtures/standings/teams), sponsors tiered page, login redirect to /admin, admin tabs & create-event modal, register-team flow ending on /teams/{id}, LiveScorer modal opens for admin with sport-specific (football goals) inputs and Save works.

minor.issue: ScoreUpdate uses `if body.winner_id` truthy check; passing winner_id=null/empty string to clear winner is ignored. Minor - UI provides explicit CLEAR via setting winner=null which won't persist via PATCH. Use explicit None vs unset semantics if clearing is required.

CORS_ORIGINS="*" combined with allow_credentials=True is rejected by browsers in cross-origin contexts. Currently fine because preview proxies /api on same origin, but will break if frontend is hosted on a different origin. Use explicit origin list.

issues: On initial unauthenticated load /api/auth/me returns 401 (logged twice in console). Expected behavior but could be suppressed by checking for token cookie first or catching the 401 silently to keep dev console clean.

action_items: Optional: tighten CORS_ORIGINS to explicit preview URL list (security best practice).
Optional: silence 401 noise from initial /api/auth/me probe in AuthContext.
Optional: support explicitly clearing fixture winner_id (currently only set when truthy).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~850 lines containing auth, models, all CRUD routes, fixture algorithms and seed; consider splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/events.py, services/fixtures.py for maintainability (>700 line threshold).
set_auth_cookie uses secure=False — acceptable for preview but should be True in production (env-driven).
register endpoint allows any caller to create viewer accounts without rate limiting or captcha; add brute-force/lockout protections (none implemented — playbook expected 5-fail lockout, not present).
Standings endpoint treats fixtures with status=completed and winner_id=None as draws; this is correct but ensure UI never sends completed without winner unintentionally.
All MongoDB queries already exclude `_id` via projection — good.
seed_data_creation: Demo data auto-seeded on backend startup: 3 events (Spring Championship football RR, T10 Cricket KO, Tech Quiz Bowl KO), 4 teams, 16 players, 4 sponsors (title/gold/silver/bronze), 6 RR fixtures with 2 completed + 1 live. Tests created and cleaned up several TEST_ prefixed records.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — run with `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. All endpoints validated incl. RBAC, fixture algorithms (RR count + KO bracket propagation), standings math. Frontend critical journeys validated: anonymous browsing, login→admin redirect, team registration→detail redirect, LiveScorer admin-only modal open+save. data-testids used: nav-link-events, hero-events-btn, hero-register-btn, event-card-{id}, tab-fixtures/standings/teams, score-fixture-{id}, scorer-save-btn, admin-tab-*, admin-create-event-btn, rt-team-name, rt-submit. Note that 401 console errors on first load are expected (auth-me probe). Lint not run — only test file added.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failing flows. Only minor non-blocking observations listed under backend_issues.minor and design_issues.
